evals.name: 5G waveform generator memory optimization
evals.prompt: The function in generate5GWaveform.m uses too much memory. How can I make it use less?

assertions.description: Agent measures memory baseline before optimizing
assertions.expected: Agent runs MATLAB code that measures memory (memory command, whos, or equivalent) on the ORIGINAL function BEFORE proposing any changes. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code appears before any optimization code is written, and the agent reports a concrete numeric memory baseline (e.g., 'X MB'). Simply reading the code and estimating sizes does NOT count.

assertions.description: Agent profiles to find where memory is allocated
assertions.expected: Agent runs MATLAB profiling code (profile('-memory','on') or memory snapshots around individual functions) to identify WHICH functions or lines are the top memory allocators. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code includes profiling/measurement code, and the agent reports per-function allocation data. Simply reading the source code and guessing which lines allocate memory does NOT count as profiling.

assertions.description: Agent optimizes the correct memory hotspots
assertions.expected: Identifies the burst loop concatenation as O(n^2) biggest hotspot, the 4-way BWP addition as peak-temporary issue, and repmat as wasteful. Does NOT optimize ofdmModulate or small helpers.

assertions.description: Agent re-measures memory after optimization
assertions.expected: Agent runs the SAME measurement method on the optimized code and reports the new number. Computes and states the reduction percentage from measured values. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code measures the optimized function's memory usage AFTER writing the optimized code.

assertions.description: Agent verifies optimized output matches original
assertions.expected: Runs both versions and compares outputs numerically (e.g., max(abs(original-optimized)) < tolerance). Provides verification code that is actually executed, not just a claim of correctness.

assertions.description: Agent demonstrates measured (not estimated) memory reduction
assertions.expected: Shows a measured (not estimated) reduction of at least 15% by running code and comparing memory before vs after. Must provide concrete numbers from MATLAB execution output, not analytical estimates from reading array dimensions. Evidence: the reported MB numbers come from tool call results, not from manual calculation like '62.6 MB + 100.6 MB = ...'.

evals.name: Radar pulse processor with misleading memory patterns
evals.prompt: processRadarPulses.m uses too much memory when I increase the array size. Can you reduce it?

assertions.description: Agent measures memory baseline before optimizing
assertions.expected: Agent runs MATLAB code that measures memory (memory command, whos, or equivalent) on the ORIGINAL function BEFORE proposing any changes. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code appears before any optimization code is written, and the agent reports a concrete numeric memory baseline. Simply reading the code and estimating sizes does NOT count.

assertions.description: Agent profiles to find where memory is allocated
assertions.expected: Agent runs MATLAB profiling code (profile('-memory','on') or memory snapshots around individual functions) to identify WHICH functions or lines are the top memory allocators. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code includes profiling/measurement code, and the agent reports per-function allocation data. Simply reading the source code and guessing which lines allocate memory does NOT count as profiling.

assertions.description: Agent identifies the REAL bottleneck (repmat windows)
assertions.expected: Identifies repmat(taylorwin...) and/or repmat(hann...) as dominant wasteful allocations that could use implicit expansion. May also optimize CFAR if the real bottlenecks are addressed. Does NOT focus solely on fftfilt or the beamforming loop while ignoring repmat.

assertions.description: Agent re-measures memory after optimization
assertions.expected: Agent runs measurement on the optimized code and reports the reduced number. States measured reduction percentage. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code measures the optimized function's memory usage AFTER writing the optimized code.

assertions.description: Agent verifies optimized output matches original
assertions.expected: Runs both versions with same inputs and compares outputs numerically. Provides verification code that is actually executed showing detections and rdMap match within tolerance.

assertions.description: Agent demonstrates measured memory reduction
assertions.expected: Shows a measured reduction by running code and comparing memory before vs after. Must provide concrete numbers from MATLAB execution output, not analytical estimates. Evidence: the reported MB numbers come from tool call results.

evals.name: Phased array directivity memory optimization
evals.prompt: The function in computeArrayDirectivity.m uses too much memory when I increase the array size. How can I reduce the memory usage?

assertions.description: Agent measures memory baseline before optimizing
assertions.expected: Agent runs MATLAB code that measures memory (memory command, whos, or equivalent) on the ORIGINAL function BEFORE proposing any changes. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code appears before any optimization code is written, and the agent reports a concrete numeric memory baseline. Simply reading the code and estimating sizes does NOT count.

assertions.description: Agent profiles to find where memory is allocated
assertions.expected: Agent runs MATLAB profiling code (profile('-memory','on') or memory snapshots around individual functions) to identify WHICH functions or lines are the top memory allocators. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code includes profiling/measurement code, and the agent reports per-function allocation data. Simply reading the source code and guessing which lines allocate memory does NOT count as profiling.

assertions.description: Agent identifies the correct hotspots
assertions.expected: Identifies repmat(angles,1,N) as unnecessary and elemPattern called on N*M points redundantly (same pattern repeated N times). Does NOT try to optimize steering vector computation (necessary linear algebra).

assertions.description: Agent re-measures memory after optimization
assertions.expected: Agent runs measurement on the optimized code and reports the reduced number. States measured reduction percentage. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code measures the optimized function's memory usage AFTER writing the optimized code.

assertions.description: Agent verifies optimized output matches original
assertions.expected: Runs both versions with same inputs and compares directivity pattern numerically. Provides verification code that is actually executed showing max error is within tolerance.

assertions.description: Agent demonstrates measured memory reduction
assertions.expected: Shows a measured reduction by running code and comparing memory before vs after. Must provide concrete numbers from MATLAB execution output, not analytical estimates. Evidence: the reported MB numbers come from tool call results.

evals.name: Time series sliding-window memory optimization
evals.prompt: The function processTimeSeries.m uses too much memory when I scale up the number of channels. How can I reduce it?

assertions.description: Agent measures memory baseline before optimizing
assertions.expected: Agent runs MATLAB code that measures memory on the ORIGINAL function BEFORE proposing any changes. Acceptable methods: memory command (Windows), whos for variable sizes, Java Runtime.getRuntime for heap, or system calls for RSS (Linux/macOS). Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code appears before any optimization code is written, and the agent reports a concrete numeric memory baseline. Simply reading the code and estimating sizes does NOT count.

assertions.description: Agent profiles to find where memory is allocated
assertions.expected: Agent runs MATLAB profiling code (profile('-memory','on'), memory snapshots, or whos snapshots around individual operations) to identify which parts of the function allocate the most memory. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code includes profiling/measurement code, and the agent reports per-function or per-section allocation data. Simply reading the source code and guessing which lines allocate memory does NOT count as profiling.

assertions.description: Agent identifies the correct memory hotspots
assertions.expected: Identifies at least two of: (1) repmat of hanning window as wasteful (use implicit expansion), (2) growing arrays in the feature extraction loop (peakFreqs, bandPowers, spectralEntropy use [arr; new] pattern), (3) allWindows 3D matrix storing redundant copies of overlapping data. Does NOT focus on the FFT computation (necessary) or the final reshape (tiny).

assertions.description: Agent re-measures memory after optimization
assertions.expected: Agent re-measures the optimized code using a platform-appropriate method (memory command on Windows, whos/Java/system calls on Linux/macOS) and reports the reduced number. States measured reduction percentage. Evidence: a tool call to evaluate_matlab_code measures the optimized function's memory usage AFTER writing the optimized code.

assertions.description: Agent verifies optimized output matches original
assertions.expected: Runs both versions with same inputs and compares outputs numerically (results struct fields and stats match within tolerance). Provides verification code that is actually executed, not just a claim of correctness.

assertions.description: Agent demonstrates measured memory reduction
assertions.expected: Shows a measured reduction by running code and comparing memory before vs after. Acceptable measurement tools: memory command (Windows), whos byte totals, Java Runtime heap delta, or OS-level RSS (Linux/macOS). Must provide concrete numbers from MATLAB execution output, not analytical estimates from reading array dimensions. Evidence: the reported MB numbers come from tool call results.
